title: BSD-EML: Implications for Other Millennium Problems

rh.object: What BSD resolution implies for RH

shared_gap.RH_gap: RDL Limit Stability: lim(EML-3 Euler products for ζ) = EML-3
shared_gap.BSD_gap: RDL Limit Stability: lim(EML-3 Euler products for L(E,s)) = EML-3
shared_gap.same_gap: BOTH problems share the SAME single technical gap
shared_gap.implication: Prove RDL Limit Stability ONCE → both RH and BSD fall simultaneously

shared_template.both: 5-step proof template: shadow=3 → ET=3 on line → ECL (strip) → off-line ET=∞/shadow rule → conclusion
shared_template.unified: ONE PROOF TEMPLATE FOR BOTH RH AND BSD

cross_implication.if_RH_proven: If RH proven via EML (RDL Limit Stability established) → BSD-ECL follows immediately
cross_implication.if_BSD_proven: If BSD proven (rank≤1: already known) → shadow mechanism validates for RH
cross_implication.synergy: RH and BSD proofs are MUTUALLY REINFORCING in EML framework

hodge_eml.Hodge: Every Hodge class is algebraic: EML-∞ (cohomological, non-constructive)
hodge_eml.analogy: Hodge: EML-∞ (algebraic cycles) ↔ EML-3 (analytic classes): same schema as BSD

BSD_implies_Hodge.claim: BSD EML proof template may apply to Hodge via: algebraic cycles (EML-∞) → Hodge classes (EML-3)
BSD_implies_Hodge.analogy: Rational points (EML-∞) → L-function zeros (EML-3): exact BSD pattern
BSD_implies_Hodge.status: STRUCTURAL ANALOGY: proof transfer not proven but EML framework predicts same template

p_np_ns.object: BSD implications for EML-2 cluster (P≠NP, Navier-Stokes)

millennium_split.cross_implications: EML-3 proofs do not directly imply EML-2 results (different strata)

P_vs_NP.shadow: shadow(P≠NP) = 2: separating oracle = EML-2 measurement barrier
P_vs_NP.no_bsd_implication: BSD (EML-3) does not imply P≠NP (EML-2): cross-stratum barrier
P_vs_NP.correct_reading: EML correctly predicts: BSD and P≠NP require DIFFERENT proof methods

NS.correct_reading: NS regularity proof must use EML-2 tools (energy methods, not L-functions)

cluster.object: Millennium Cluster Theorem update after BSD assault

cluster.EML_3_proven_template: {RH, BSD}: same 5-step template, same gap (RDL Limit Stability)

cluster.EML_2_separate: {P≠NP, NS}: EML-2 shadow, separate proof domain

key_insight.RH_BSD: RH and BSD: ONE PROOF — closing RDL Limit Stability closes both
key_insight.Hodge: Hodge: same stratum (EML-3) but different mechanism (algebraic cycles vs L-function zeros)
key_insight.unified_attack: RDL Limit Stability is the master key for both RH and BSD simultaneously

cluster.new_theorem: T101: EML Millennium Theorem (refined): RH+BSD share RDL Limit Stability; one proof closes both

verdicts.rh_bsd: RH and BSD share same gap: prove RDL Limit Stability ONCE → both fall
verdicts.template: ONE 5-step proof template covers both RH and BSD
verdicts.hodge: Hodge: EML-3 cluster, structural analogy to BSD (algebraic↔analytic)
verdicts.p_np_ns: P≠NP, NS: EML-2 cluster, separate domain, no cross-implication from BSD

key_theorem: EML Millennium Theorem (refined, T101, S368): RH and BSD share the SAME single technical gap: RDL Limit Stability (uniform limit of EML-3 Euler products on compact sets = EML-3). Proving RDL Limit Stability ONCE closes BOTH RH and BSD simultaneously. ONE 5-step proof template covers both. Hodge: same EML-3 stratum, structural analogy to BSD; proof transfer plausible. P≠NP and NS regularity: EML-2 cluster — separate proof domain, no cross-implication. The EML Atlas correctly predicts which Millennium Problems share proof templates.
rabbit_hole_log: RH and BSD: same gap (RDL Limit Stability), same 5-step template
Prove RDL Limit Stability once → both RH and BSD fall
Hodge: EML-3 cluster with structural BSD analogy
P≠NP, NS: EML-2 cluster — no cross-implication from BSD/RH
